message:
feat(optim): add LambdaLR, SequentialLR, and ChainedScheduler LR schedulers

Implement three new learning rate scheduler types that mirror their
PyTorch counterparts, completing the standard scheduler suite:

- LambdaLR: applies a user-provided closure as a multiplier to the base
  learning rate each epoch, with negative multiplier clamping to zero
  for safety. Supports builder-style .last_epoch() and .verbose()
  configuration.

- SequentialLR: switches between an ordered list of sub-schedulers at
  configurable milestone epochs. Each phase receives a local epoch
  count relative to its start, enabling warmup-to-decay pipelines
  (e.g., LinearLR warmup followed by CosineAnnealingLR).

- ChainedScheduler: composes multiple schedulers multiplicatively by
  running each against a ShadowOptimizer seeded with the base LR and
  accumulating their scaling factors. This allows combining independent
  decay policies (e.g., StepLR * ExponentialLR).

Also adds a private ShadowOptimizer helper struct used internally by
ChainedScheduler to isolate per-scheduler LR computation without
mutating the real optimizer during the composition step.

All three schedulers implement the full LRScheduler trait including
state_dict/load_state_dict for checkpoint serialization. Test coverage
includes:
- LambdaLR: linear warmup, step decay, negative clamp, state round-trip
- SequentialLR: milestone transitions, single-scheduler equivalence,
  state round-trip
- ChainedScheduler: multiplicative composition, single-scheduler
  equivalence, state round-trip

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
